sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: FC Arouca hosts Casa Pia in Round 32 of the league at the Estádio Municipal de Arouca in Arouca! This matchup pits a struggling FC Arouca, currently in 12th position with 34 points, against a relatively stronger Casa Pia, who sit in 8th with 42 points. With only a few rounds left, both teams are eager to secure valuable points, though their objectives differ. FC Arouca will look to climb away from the lower half of the table, while Casa Pia aims to solidify their mid-table standing or push for a higher finish. The stakes are high in this encounter as both teams have shown inconsistency in recent performances.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at their last 5 matchups, Casa Pia holds a clear edge with 3 wins compared to FC Arouca’s 1 win and 1 draw! Their most recent encounter on December 20, 2024, saw Casa Pia triumph 3-1 at home, with Leonardo Lelo earning the Player of the Match title with an impressive 8.6 Sofascore rating. Interestingly, head-to-head trends show a pattern of low-scoring games, with 4 out of the last 5 matches featuring under 2.5 goals. FC Arouca has also struggled to keep clean sheets against Casa Pia, failing to do so in their last 3 encounters. This history suggests Casa Pia might have a psychological advantage going into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: FC Arouca comes into this match with some concerning trends, having been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 home games! Their matches also tend to feature fewer corners, with 7 out of their last 8 home games having under 10.5 corners. On the other side, Casa Pia is currently on a 4-game streak without a win, which could be a cause for concern for the away side. They’ve also been the first to concede in 7 of their last 8 away matches, showing defensive vulnerabilities on the road. Additionally, Casa Pia’s games often see high disciplinary action, with over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 matches, aligning with their physical style of play.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players to Watch
sections.text: For FC Arouca, Jason stands out as a key creative force in midfield with 12 big chances created, ranking 5th in the league for this stat! Another player to watch is Boris Popović, a defender who dominates ground duels with a 66.7% win rate, ranking 2nd in the league. Up front, Henrique Araújo has contributed 5 goals, though his expected goals of 5.77 suggest he could be more clinical. For Casa Pia, Cassiano is a major threat with 10 goals this season, placing him 5th in the league scoring charts. Leonardo Lelo, with 4 assists and a 7.08 average rating, also remains a pivotal figure in midfield after his stellar performance in their last meeting.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Style
sections.text: FC Arouca boasts a respectable 52.59% average ball possession, ranking 6th in the league, and excels in creating opportunities with 70 big chances created, also ranking 5th! Their passing accuracy stands at 81.55%, placing them 7th, which suggests they can control games when at their best. However, they’ve only converted 35.71% of big chances, ranking 11th, highlighting finishing issues. Casa Pia, on the other hand, has shown resilience in attack, with players like Cassiano capitalizing on chances. Their goal distribution shows strength in the 31-45 and 76-90 minute periods, each accounting for 24.39% of their goals, indicating they often score before halftime and late in games.

sections.subtitle: Defensive and Disciplinary Insights
sections.text: Defensively, FC Arouca has conceded 47 goals this season, ranking 13th for least goals conceded, and struggles with big chances missed by opponents, with 69 created against them! They commit a high number of fouls, totaling 466 (6th most in the league), and have accumulated 84 yellow cards, ranking 12th for least cards. Casa Pia’s defense has also shown cracks, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 away games. With referee Vasilica Iancu officiating, who averages 4.8 yellow cards per game, we can expect a potentially fiery encounter given both teams’ tendencies for physical play.

sections.subtitle: Key Tactical Battles
sections.text: One intriguing battle will be between FC Arouca’s Jason, with his knack for creating big chances, and Casa Pia’s defensive setup led by veterans like José Fonte! Another area to watch is how Casa Pia’s Cassiano exploits FC Arouca’s defense, which has been prone to conceding early as seen in recent streaks. In midfield, Leonardo Lelo’s ability to deliver assists for Casa Pia could test FC Arouca’s Morlaye Sylla, who has underperformed his expected goals of 5.54 with just 2 scored. Additionally, FC Arouca’s high ranking in tackles per game (16.19, 5th in the league) could disrupt Casa Pia’s rhythm if they press effectively.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Records
sections.text: Did you know FC Arouca has hit the woodwork 12 times this season, ranking 6th among the unluckiest teams in the league? Their goals vs. expected goals difference of 8.67 is the best in the league for overperformance, showing they’ve exceeded expectations in attack! Casa Pia’s standout player from past matchups against FC Arouca, goalkeeper Ricardo Batista, has an average Sofascore rating of 7.375 over 4 starts, with 3 saves per game, though he hasn’t scored or assisted. Also, FC Arouca scores 23.4% of their goals in the 76-90 minute period, hinting at potential late drama in this match.
